LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANTONIO VILLALOBOS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. GRANTED. MR. VILLALOBOS MAY FILE HIS PROPOSED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 22, 2017. GRANTING THE MOTION COMPORTS WITH CALIFORNIA'S POLICY OF LIBERALLY ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS AND THERE IS NO DISCERNABLE LEGAL PREJUDICE TO ANY PARTY BY THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION. DEFENDANT UNITED RENTALS (NORTH AMERICA), INC.'S OBJECTIONS TO THE PROPOSED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ARE BEST ADDRESSED ON THE MERITS BY A DEMURRER, SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION OR AT TRIAL, NOT A MOTION TO AMEND. ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT TO BE FILED. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Set For Trial On Sep-24-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Mar-05-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joshua D. White, Esq. of JONES CLIFFORD, Tel. No. 415.431.5310, appearing for Plaintiffs Antonio Villalobos, et al.;
Charles S. Custer, Esq. of GORDON & REES LLP, Tel. No. 415.986.5900, appearing for Defendant United Rentals.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem.

Ruling: Argument held. Plaintiffs Antonio Villalobos and Gina Delgado's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents is submitted by counsel. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as modified: The motion to compel production is granted. No showing of good cause was required and, if it were, moving party made an ample showing. Opposing party failed to establish that the variously-identified document in question constitutes an attorney-client communication or is subject to the work product doctrine. Among other things, opposing party failed to provide a code-compliant privilege log, failed to identify the document withheld with specificity but only by generic description ("report"), failed to identify the individual author as required by law but only the apparent author's employer, and failed to establish that the dominant purpose of the document was communication between a client and an attorney. Opposing party provided no sworn information as to the genesis of the document and asserted in the privilege log that the document had been prepared by an apparent non-attorney and communicated only to the insured. From opposing party's documents, it is not even possible to establish whether and when the document was ever transmitted to an attorney. Sanctions were not requested by the moving party and will not be awarded. The documents are to be produced within 10 days of the service of this order. Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. (302/JPT/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory No. 17.1 And Request For Admissions, Set One
Off Calendar Feb-14-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Charles E. Custer, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP, T:415.986.5900, for defendant/moving party;
Joshua D. White, Esq. of Jones Clifford, T:415.431.5310, for plaintiff.

Both parties stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT UNITED RENTALS (NORTH AMERICA) INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory No. 17.1 And Requests For Admissions, Nos. 11 - 18 Set One is DENIED. The purpose of requests for admissions is to narrow the issues, but the court lacks the power to require a party to admit a request if it chooses to either deny or assert an inability to either admit or deny. Whether on not plaintiff's response to Form Interrogatory 17.1 is sufficient to justify plaintiff refusal to admit the requested admissions will have to await trial.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Feb-14-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Charles E. Custer, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP, T:415.986.5900, for defendant/moving party;
Joshua D. White, Esq. of Jones Clifford, T:415.431.5310, for plaintiff.

Both parties stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT UNITED RENTALS (NORTH AMERICA) INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents is granted as follows:
1. Plaintiff's objections as to requests nos. 21- 38 are overruled;
2. As to requests Nos 21, 22, 25 - 29 & 38 -plaintiff is ordered to respond within twenty (20) days from the date of this order identifying the responsive documents in his possession, if any, which he asserts are also in the possession of defendant.
3. Plaintiff's responses to requests Nos 23, 24 and 30 -37 are sufficiently responsive in that plaintiff responded that it has no responsive documents in his possession.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Ross J. Psyhogios of the law offices of Jones Clifford (888-625-2251) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF ANTONIO VILLALOBOS' Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. Mr. Villalobos may file his proposed first amended complaint no later than November 22, 2017. Granting the motion comports with California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings and there is no discernable legal prejudice to any party by the granting of the motion. Defendant United Rentals (North America), Inc.'s objections to the proposed first amended complaint are best addressed on the merits by a demurrer, summary judgment motion or at trial, not a motion to amend. Answer to first amended complaint to be filed. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Sep-25-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Ross J. Psyhogios of the law offices of JC Jones Clifford (415-431-5310) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, Antonio Villalobos

Attorney, Carla Vasquez Dos Santos-Moore of the law offices of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara LLP (510-540-4881) appears by telephone as counsel for defendant, South City Tow, Inc.

Attorney, David A. Serrano of the law offices of Gordon & Rees LLP (415-986-5900) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, United Rentals (North America), Inc.

Attorney, Bruce E. Pitcaithley of the law offices of Cohen & Associates (510-645-4680) appears by telephone as counsel for Intervenor, San Francisco Housing Authority

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT UNITED RENTALS (NORTH AMERICA), INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS as to the complaint filed by plaintiff Antonio Villalobos is DENIED. Liberally construing the complaint, Mr. Villalobos has adequately alleged that United Rentals is liable for the negligence of its agent defendant South City Tow, Inc. Liability based on negligence of an owner of rental property that was committed by an owner's agent that is imputed to the owner under California law is not precluded by the Graves Amendment. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)
